title: Bridge Essay: Cognitive and behavioural correlates of psychosis in genetic subtypes of Prader-Willi syndrome.
concept: Emptiness from predictive processing — the self as inference, dependent arising as Bayes-optimal perception.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Dependent Arising** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Emptiness from predictive processing — the self as inference, dependent arising as Bayes-optimal perception.

**Cognitive and behavioural correlates of psychosis in genetic subtypes of Prader-Willi syndrome.** - Journal of psychiatric research (2026). DOI: 10.1016/j.jpsychires.2026.06.054. PMID: 42407272. BACKGROUND: Prader-Willi Syndrome (PWS) is a rare neurodevelopmental disorder resulting from the loss of paternally expressed, maternally imprinted genes within the chromosome 15q11-q13 region. While core features such as hyperphagia and developmental delay are nearly universal, the maternal uniparental disomy (mUPD) subtype carries a significantly higher risk of psychosis compared to the paternal deletion subtype.
